--- SOURCE 1: A Closer Look At The Growth In Ad-Supported Streaming - Antenna ---
URL: https://www.antenna.live/insights/a-closer-look-at-the-growth-in-ad-supported-streaming

SUMMARY:
When looking at the share of Gross Additions by plan tier in May 2024, Antenna observed growth in ad-supported tiers across all SVOD services that offer them when compared to the same period in 2023. According to Antenna data, only Peacock (**70%**) and Hulu (**61%**) had a majority of Gross Additions (meaning new paying Subscribers, including those converted from trials) opting in for ad-supported plans in May 2023. Antenna observed **39%** of Netflix’s Gross Adds went to ad-supported plans in May 2024, an increase of **+17pts** compared to the same period in 2023. Two years ago, when ad-supported streaming was still finding its footing, we introduced Antenna’s Ad Choice segmentation to help the industry gain clarity around consumer choice related to ad-supported Premium SVOD and how these decisions change over time.

Insights

A Closer Look At The Growth In Ad-Supported Streaming
=====================================================

A popular topic of discussion at this year’s Cannes Lions Festival of Creativity, which officially wraps up today, was advertising on streaming platforms. As linear television audiences continue to decline, causing [a choppy ad market](https://www.wsj.com/business/media/streaming-was-supposed-to-rescue-the-ailing-tv-ad-business-it-hasnt-9fa2d855), there are real questions about whether ad-supported streaming can make up the difference.

But there are reasons to be optimistic about the future of ad-supported streaming. When looking at the share of Gross Additions by plan tier in May 2024, Antenna observed growth in ad-supported tiers across all SVOD services that offer them when compared to the same period in 2023.

![Image 1](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6756b521db09e280168a4cf3/6797c23d5b685c74fc7a943f_67658c7baa3d1103ea9f79b9_66759f4375e32608b2db742c_BLOG%252520FIGURE%252520PLAN%252520TIER.png)

According to Antenna data, only Peacock (**70%**) and Hulu (**61%**) had a majority of Gross Additions (meaning new paying Subscribers, including those converted from trials) opting in for ad-supported plans in May 2023. Fast-forward to May 2024 and the list of services with a majority of Gross Adds going to ad-supported plans expands to include Peacock (**74%**), Hulu (**63%**), Discovery+ (**58%**), Disney+ (**58%**), and Paramount+ (**55%**).

Max nearly doubled its share of ad-supported Gross Adds to **46%** in May 2024 compared to **24%**in May 2023. Antenna observed **39%** of Netflix’s Gross Adds went to ad-supported plans in May 2024, an increase of **+17pts** compared to the same period in 2023. And AMC+ and BET+, which didn’t offer ad-supported tier in May 2023, saw **39%** and **10%**, respectively, of its Gross Adds going to ad-supported plans.

While it remains to be seen whether streaming advertising will make up for the lost inventory and revenue of television, streaming services are gaining real traction with their ad plans.

For more detailed information on Antenna’s methodology and definitions of core metrics, please visit [http://www.antenna.live/methodology](http://www.antenna.live/methodology).

#### Other insights

[![Image 3](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6756b521db09e280168a4cf3/6874f90c152984f983f1db02_A%20Whole%20New%20Netflix%20thumbnail.png) [Vertical](https://www.antenna.live/insight-category/vertical) [Subscriber Metrics](https://www.antenna.live/insight-category/subscriber-metrics) A Whole New Netflix Netflix may be the “legacy” video streamer, but they are far from complacent. Netflix’s complexion today is markedly changed from the Netflix of three years ago. In recent years they have implemented a number of significant changes, including: Password-sharing crackdown; Ad tier launch; Live events, and Price increases. See More](https://www.antenna.live/insights/a-whole-new-netflix)

[![Image 4](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6756b521db09e280168a4cf3/68657917a0395380a5636f7f_How%20Third-Party%20Distribution%20is%20Fueling%20SVOD%20Growth%20THUMBNAIL.png) [Packaging & Distribution](https://www.antenna.live/insight-category/packaging-distribution) How Third-Party Distribution is Fueling SVOD Growth Third-party distribution has become a significant part of the growth story for streaming video, particularly “channels” environments. Antenna estimates that 1 in 3 new streaming subscriptions in Q1'25 came through these stores—a sharp increase from just two years prior, with the vast majority via Amazon Prime Video Channels. See More](https://www.antenna.live/insights/how-third-party-distribution-is-fueling-svod-growth)

[![Image 5](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6756b521db09e280168a4cf3/684aff9ced454dfc40f15289_Premium%20SVOD%20Segmentation%20Blog%20Post%20thumbnail.png) [State of Subscriptions](https://www.antenna.live/insight-category/state-of-subscriptions) [Vertical](https://www.antenna.live/insight-category/vertical) [Subscriber Audiences](https://www.antenna.live/insight-category/subscriber-audiences) To Ad or Not To Ad? Two years ago, when ad-supported streaming was still finding its footing, we introduced Antenna’s Ad Choice segmentation to help the industry gain clarity around consumer choice related to ad-supported Premium SVOD and how these decisions change over time. We developed four different segment profiles: “Ad Avoiders,” “Ad Engagers,” “Ad Managers,” and “Ad Oblivious.” See More](https://www.antenna.live/insights/to-ad-or-not-to-ad)

--- SOURCE 2: New Research Shows Ad-Supported Streaming TV Is Growing FAST ---
URL: https://www.adweek.com/convergent-tv/what-advertisers-need-to-know-about-fast-channels/

SUMMARY:
Free ad-supported streaming channels in key markets have nearly doubled since mid-2023, according to Nielsen's Gracenote.

FULL CONTENT:
### [Streaming & OTT](https://www.adweek.com/category/streaming-ott/)

# New Research Shows Ad-Supported Streaming TV Is Growing FAST

## Free ad-supported streaming channels in key markets have nearly doubled since mid-2023, according to Nielsen's Gracenote

![image of Gracenote logo on TV](https://static-www.adweek.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/gracenote-evolving-fast-landscape-2025.jpg?w=1200 "gracenote-evolving-fast-landscape-2025")
![Headshot of Saleah Blancaflor](https://static-prod.adweek.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/07/saleah-headshot.jpeg)

The world of FAST is changing, well… fast, according to a new Gracenote report.

Today, Gracenote, Nielsen’s content data business unit, released new research, data, and insights on the evolving landscape of free ad-supported TV services (FAST), providing information for distribution platforms and advertisers seeking to monetize consumer engagement and understand the shifting [streaming](https://www.adweek.com/category/streaming-ott/) environment.

According to the research, which taps [Gracenote’s Global Video Data](https://gracenote.com/products/video-data/), the number of active FAST channels in key markets, including the United States, United Kingdom, Germany, and Canada, have nearly doubled since mid-2023 to more than 1,610.

In addition, nearly three-fourths of the available FAST channels are in the United States, which is the leading FAST market.

![Headshot of Saleah Blancaflor](https://static-prod.adweek.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/07/saleah-headshot.jpeg)

#### [Saleah Blancaflor](https://www.adweek.com/contributor/saleah-blancaflor/) Saleah Blancaflor is a TV reporter at ADWEEK. [@saleyley](https://twitter.com/saleyley)| [saleah.blancaflor@adweek.com](mailto:saleah.blancaflor@adweek.com)

Saleah Blancaflor is a TV reporter at ADWEEK.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Ad-supported streaming is surging, with services like Netflix and Hulu seeing significant subscriber shifts towards ad-supported tiers, driven by cost-conscious viewers.
Emerging technologies such as AI enhance content personalization, while 5G enables high-quality streaming, broadening accessibility and improving user experiences on mobile devices.
Live streaming is becoming increasingly popular, with platforms integrating real-time content to engage audiences, driving viewership and creating new advertising opportunities.
